steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The father is trying to find me among some other people, which shows he is looking for the speaker in a crowd.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The word "find" is used when the father is searching for the narrator in a crowd. The meaning needs to be spot or identify someone's location. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — ‘Locate’: to discover the position or whereabouts of — fits the father scanning a crowd to spot the narrator among others. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Affect’: to have an impact or influence on — describes causing a change in someone, not searching for them in a crowd. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Reveal’: to make known or disclose — implies the father is uncovering hidden information, not searching for a person's physical location. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Criticize’: to judge negatively — has nothing to do with searching for someone in a crowd. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
